api/spec/packages/aip.purpose: Root package for the v3 AIP TypeSpec spec: compiles domain operation interfaces into api/v3/openapi.yaml via two root namespace files (openmeter.tsp, konnect.tsp) plus post-processing scripts, and enforces naming/structural constraints via a custom linter in lib/. All route binding, tag metadata, and security schemes live here; domain model definitions do not.

patterns.name: Two-step compile pipeline
patterns.description: tsp compile produces OpenAPI YAML, then scripts/flatten-allof.mjs normalises allOf schemas and seal-object-schemas.mjs closes object schemas. Both run via `pnpm generate` (package.json). Never hand-edit the output.
patterns.example: pnpm generate runs: tsp compile --config tspconfig.yaml ./src && node ./scripts/flatten-allof.mjs ... && node ./scripts/seal-object-schemas.mjs ...

patterns.name: Route and tag binding at root only
patterns.description: @route and @tag are declared exclusively in src/openmeter.tsp and src/konnect.tsp, never inside domain sub-folder operation files. Adding @route inside a domain operations.tsp silently duplicates routes.

patterns.name: Domain ops imported via index.tsp barrels
patterns.description: Each domain sub-folder exposes an index.tsp barrel; root files import only the barrel, not individual operation files. This prevents accidental double-imports.

patterns.name: Security via @useRef to external YAML
patterns.description: Security schemes are TypeSpec model stubs with @useRef pointing to external YAML fragments in common/definitions/, not inline credential definitions.

patterns.name: Custom linter via createRule factory
patterns.description: All lint rules in lib/rules/ use the TypeSpec createRule factory and are registered in lib/index.js $linter export. Error severity blocks gen-api; warning does not. New rules have zero effect until registered in lib/index.js.
patterns.example: lib/rules/casing.js: export const casingRule = createRule({ name: 'casing', severity: 'warning', ... }); registered in lib/index.js rules array.

patterns.name: omit-unreachable-types: true in tspconfig
patterns.description: tspconfig.yaml sets omit-unreachable-types: true so only reachable schemas appear in emitted OpenAPI. Also sets seal-object-schemas: true. Both must remain set.
patterns.example: tspconfig.yaml options: '@typespec/openapi3': omit-unreachable-types: true; seal-object-schemas: true

patterns.name: PascalCase acronym exceptions in utils.js
patterns.description: Accepted uppercase acronyms (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID) live exclusively in lib/rules/utils.js pascalCaseExceptions. Never add exceptions inline in individual rule files.

key_file_guides.role: Compilation entry point; imports openmeter.tsp and konnect.tsp to produce two distinct OpenAPI outputs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing import here silently drops a namespace from all output artifacts.

key_file_guides.role: Binds all domain interfaces to @route paths and @tag metadata for the OpenMeter self-hosted variant.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new domain import without a matching @tagMetadata declaration silently omits the tag description from generated OpenAPI.

key_file_guides.role: Konnect variant root; overrides security schemes and some sub-routes relative to openmeter.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Inline security scheme bodies here duplicate config; always use @useRef to common/definitions/security.yaml instead.

key_file_guides.role: Post-processes emitted YAML to move sibling properties into allOf branches so SDK generators can consume them.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Removing the x-flatten-allOf idempotency marker check causes infinite re-run loops; changing YAML_OPTIONS.lineWidth from 0 creates spurious line-wrap diffs in committed openapi.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Rewrites additionalProperties: {not:{}} to false to preserve closed-schema semantics after TypeSpec compilation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not remove — without this step object schemas are not properly sealed and SDK generators may generate overly permissive types.

key_file_guides.role: Registers all linter rules via defineLinter/$linter export consumed by TypeSpec compiler during gen-api.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new rule file has zero effect until its export is added to the rules array here.

key_file_guides.role: Centralised casing helpers and PascalCase acronym exception list shared across all lint rules.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Duplicating regex or exception lists in individual rule files causes divergence from the canonical set.

key_file_guides.role: Compiler config: emitter output dir, omit-unreachable-types, seal-object-schemas, linter extends.
key_file_guides.watch_for: omit-unreachable-types and seal-object-schemas must stay true; linter extends must reference @openmeter/api-spec-aip/all.

anti_patterns: Declaring @route or @tag inside a domain sub-folder's operations.tsp — routing is bound only in src/openmeter.tsp and src/konnect.tsp
Hand-editing api/v3/openapi.yaml or api/v3/api.gen.go — always regenerate via `make gen-api` then `make generate`
Defining a lint rule without registering it in lib/index.js — the rule is compiled but has zero effect at runtime
Adding new accepted PascalCase acronyms anywhere other than pascalCaseExceptions in lib/rules/utils.js
Adding a domain import in src/openmeter.tsp without a matching @tagMetadata declaration — tag description is silently absent from generated OpenAPI

decisions.decision: Two root namespace files (openmeter.tsp, konnect.tsp) instead of one parameterised file
decisions.rationale: The two variants differ in security schemes and some sub-route overrides; TypeSpec has no preprocessor so conditional logic would require complex emitter workarounds. Separate files keep the variants explicit.

decisions.decision: Post-processing scripts run after tsp compile, not as TypeSpec emitter plugins
decisions.rationale: TypeSpec emitter produces allOf schemas with sibling properties that confuse SDK generators, and additionalProperties: {not:{}} that need rewriting. These are output-level fixups that cannot be done inside the TypeSpec compiler without patching upstream.

decisions.decision: Error vs warning severity split in the linter
decisions.rationale: Only structurally breaking rules (friendlyName for SDK-visible names, composition-over-inheritance) use error severity to block gen-api. Stylistic rules (casing, docs, field-prefix) use warning to surface guidance without blocking developer flow.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new domain resource to the v3 spec

api/spec/packages/legacy.purpose: TypeSpec source for the v1/v2 OpenMeter REST API and OpenMeter Cloud API; compiles to api/openapi.yaml, api/openapi.cloud.yaml, and the Python SDK. src/ is the composition root with shared primitives (types.tsp, errors.tsp, filter.tsp); cloud/main.tsp is the cloud-variant entry point. New v1 domain features go here; v3 changes go to api/spec/packages/aip.

patterns.name: cloud/main.tsp as sole OpenAPI compilation entry point
patterns.description: All sub-domain .tsp files must be imported into src/cloud/main.tsp. Files not listed are silently excluded from api/openapi.cloud.yaml. src/main.tsp is the separate entry for Python SDK generation.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every named type
patterns.description: Every model, enum, union, and interface must have @friendlyName controlling the OpenAPI schema name and SDK class name. The linter enforces this at error severity — missing @friendlyName blocks gen-api.

patterns.name: @visibility lifecycle decorators on model fields
patterns.description: Use Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update to control which fields appear in which request/response shapes from a single model definition instead of defining separate request/response models.

patterns.name: REST resource templates for CRUD request bodies
patterns.description: Derive request body models from TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceCreateModel<T>, ResourceReplaceModel<T>, ResourceCreateOrUpdateModel<T>. Do not define ad-hoc request body types.

patterns.name: Shared primitives in types.tsp — never re-declared
patterns.description: ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource, ResourceTimestamps, CadencedResource, and all pagination models live in src/types.tsp under the OpenMeter namespace. Sub-domain files import from there; never re-declare these types.
patterns.example: // Sub-domain file uses: OpenMeter.ULID — not a local re-declaration

patterns.name: @extension("x-omitempty", true) on all filter operator fields
patterns.description: Every operator property in filter models must carry @extension("x-omitempty", true) so absent filters are excluded from serialised query strings.

patterns.name: @operationId on every interface operation
patterns.description: Explicit camelCase verb+noun @operationId prevents non-deterministic SDK function names across Go, JS, and Python clients.

key_file_guides.role: Cloud-variant compilation entry point; imports all sub-domain files to produce api/openapi.cloud.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New sub-domain file not imported here is silently excluded from api/openapi.cloud.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Client-emit entry point for Python SDK generation via tspconfig.client.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Separate from cloud/main.tsp; new domains must be added to both to keep OpenAPI and Python SDK in sync.

key_file_guides.role: Shared primitive types: ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource, pagination models. Single source of truth for all shared types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never re-declare these in sub-domain files; any duplication creates divergent $ref targets in generated OpenAPI.

key_file_guides.role: OpenMeterError and error union types with @error decorator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Extending Error without @error decorator breaks OpenAPI error schema generation — error responses will not be tagged correctly.

key_file_guides.role: Generic filter operator models; every field must have @extension("x-omitempty", true).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing x-omitempty causes empty filter fields to be serialised as null in query strings, breaking filter semantics.

key_file_guides.role: Registers all v1 linter rules via defineLinter/$linter export.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New rule file has zero effect without registration here; no compile error signals the omission.

key_file_guides.role: OpenAPI emitter config; output dir must match Makefile expectations for api/openapi.cloud.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing emitter-output-dir breaks the `make gen-api` path expectations and the CI dirty-tree check.

key_file_guides.role: Python SDK emitter config; points to api/client/python output directory.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both tspconfig files extend @openmeter/api-spec-legacy/all linter; removing this breaks linting for whichever emit run omits it.

anti_patterns: Defining new domain models directly in root-level .tsp files — create a sub-folder with its own main.tsp instead
Re-declaring primitive types (ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource) in sub-domain files — they already exist in src/types.tsp
Using `extends Error` without the @error decorator — breaks OpenAPI error schema generation
Omitting @operationId on interface operations — causes non-deterministic generated SDK function names
Adding new sub-domain files without registering them in both src/cloud/main.tsp and src/main.tsp imports

decisions.decision: Two separate tspconfig files (tspconfig.yaml for OpenAPI, tspconfig.client.yaml for Python SDK)
decisions.rationale: OpenAPI and Python SDK emitters need different output directories and emitter-specific options; a single config cannot parameterise both without complex per-run overrides.

decisions.decision: Shared primitive types centralised in types.tsp with Resource base models
decisions.rationale: Keeps schema naming deterministic across all sub-domains and prevents duplicate $ref targets in generated OpenAPI that confuse SDK generators and produce mismatched type names.

decisions.decision: friendlyNameRule is error-severity (blocks gen-api); all other rules are warnings
decisions.rationale: @friendlyName controls SDK class names across Go, JS, and Python; a missing or wrong name produces an irreversible public API breakage, justifying a hard block on compilation.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v1 resource (Widget) with CRUD operations
